blocks.text: Melissa Johnson, with Jason Torres copied:

A short update on where we are and one thing I need from your team. The order and cost figures your office sent came through cleanly, and I have started laying out how a web order moves through {{fact:f:E-2011-001.client}} from the moment a customer places it. The picture is taking shape: the hand-off between the group that takes the order and the group that fills it is where most of the delay collects, which lines up with what your managers told us in the first interviews.

The one gap is the customer-service side. I have the order data and the fulfillment data, but not the record of the questions and complaints raised against those orders, and that record is what tells us whether a problem is a system fault or an ownership gap. If someone on your team can pull the last quarter of service tickets tied to online orders by the end of the week, we stay on schedule for the working session. Jason will be on site Thursday and can take anything that is easier to hand over in person than to send.

Nothing else is needed from you today. I will send the draft order-path map ahead of the session so you are not seeing it cold in the room.

Deborah Allen
